page: Microsoft Azure M16-4ms is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MS server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8280M processor operating at 2.7 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 4 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and 2 threads per core. A key characteristic of this server is its massive memory allocation of 438.0 GB, yielding 109.38 GB of RAM per core. Additionally, it is equipped with 524 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include any GPUs.

Benchmark results indicate that the M16-4ms prioritizes memory capacity over raw processing speed. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance score is 1033.09, and the multi-core score is 2722.46, placing both in the lower performance tiers. Geekbench scores further reflect this trend, with a single-core score of 884.0 and a multi-core score of 1961.0. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show a read-only small block cached speed of 151.48 GB/sec, while uncached large block read and write speeds are 18.75 GB/sec and 15.92 GB/sec respectively. Redis SET operations achieve 2.69 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput reaches 3.12 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the M16-4ms offers high memory density relative to its virtual CPU count, making it a specialized choice for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive CPU calculations. The primary tradeoff is the lower CPU and memory bandwidth performance relative to the massive RAM capacity. This server is best suited for large in-memory databases, caching systems, and memory-heavy data processing tasks where RAM capacity is the primary constraint rather than computational throughput.
description: Microsoft Azure M16-4ms is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the MS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8280M processor at 2.7 GHz, it provides 4 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and a massive 438.0 GB of RAM, resulting in 109.38 GB of memory per core. The server includes 524 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, with no GPU resources. Benchmark data shows weak CPU and memory bandwidth performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 1961.0 and uncached large block read speeds of 18.75 GB/sec. Consequently, the M16-4ms represents a qualitative tradeoff, offering extreme memory density at the expense of computational speed. It is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads such as large in-memory databases, caching layers, and data processing tasks that demand substantial RAM capacity but minimal CPU processing power.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M16-4ms is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs, 438.0 GB of RAM, and 524 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads with low CPU demands.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M16-4ms is a memory-optimized VM with 4 vCPUs, 438.0 GB of RAM, and 524 GB of local storage, running on Intel Xeon processors.
tagline: A memory-intensive virtual machine featuring massive RAM capacity and local storage for memory-bound workloads with low compute requirements.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8280M processor with 4 vCPUs and 2 physical cores
Massive 438.0 GB RAM capacity providing 109.38 GB per core
524 GB local storage with Premium Storage capability
Optimized for memory-bound workloads with low CPU compute requirements
Well-suited for large in-memory databases and caching layers
